cb_step_1.title: Choose Pelvic Volume
cb_step_1.simple: Select the pelvic CT volume as the input.
cb_step_1.detailed: The pelvic CT volume is the foundation for all subsequent segmentation and planning. It provides the anatomical reference for the entire procedure. In the dropdown, pick the volume that best covers the pelvis and fracture fragments.

cb_step_2.title: Segment Pelvis
cb_step_2.simple: Click the button to automatically segment the pelvic bone.
cb_step_2.detailed: This step uses the CT volume to automatically generate a segmentation of the pelvic bone, separating it from soft tissues. The resulting mask will be used to identify major bone fragments and guide fracture segmentation.

cb_step_3.title: Segment Fractures
cb_step_3.simple: Click to automatically detect and segment the fracture lines.
cb_step_3.detailed: This step identifies the fracture gaps within the pelvic segmentation, creating separate segments for each bone fragment. Accurate fracture segmentation is critical for later reduction and screw planning.

cb_step_4.title: Exclude Fragments
cb_step_4.simple: Untick any fragments you do not want to include in the reduction plan.
cb_step_4.detailed: Review the automatically segmented fragments. Uncheck those that are not clinically relevant (e.g., small avulsion fragments) to simplify the template generation and reduction planning. Only checked fragments will be used in subsequent steps.

cb_step_5.title: Generate Template
cb_step_5.simple: Click to create a preliminary reduction template based on the selected fragments.
cb_step_5.detailed: This step analyzes the fracture configuration and generates an initial reduction template that attempts to bring the fragments back into anatomical alignment. The template serves as a starting point for the registration and manual adjustments that follow.

cb_step_6.title: Register and Reduce
cb_step_6.simple: Click to automatically align and reduce the fracture fragments.
cb_step_6.detailed: This step applies an automated registration algorithm to reposition the fragments according to the template, achieving a preliminary reduction. The resulting segmentation shows the fragments in their planned postoperative positions.

cb_step_7.title: Manual Adjustment? (Optional)
cb_step_7.simple: Tick the checkbox if you need to refine the position of any fragment manually, or leave unticked to skip.
cb_step_7.detailed: After automated reduction, you may want to fine-tune individual fragment positions for optimal anatomical alignment. Ticking this box will enable manual adjustment tools. If you are satisfied with the automated result, skip directly to screw planning.

cb_step_8.title: Select Fragment to Adjust
cb_step_8.simple: Pick the fragment you wish to manually reposition from the dropdown list.
cb_step_8.detailed: This step lets you focus on a specific bone fragment that requires manual correction. Selecting it will activate the transform tools for that fragment only, allowing precise adjustments without affecting other fragments.

cb_step_9.title: Adjust Fragment Position
cb_step_9.simple: Use the 3D view to drag and rotate the selected fragment into better alignment.
cb_step_9.detailed: Fine-tune the placement of the fragment using the interactive transform controls in the 3D viewer. Adjust position and rotation until the fragment matches the anatomical contours and restores the natural pelvic ring. Your adjustments will be saved for the final plan.

cb_step_10.title: Apply Adjustments
cb_step_10.simple: Click to confirm the manual adjustments made to the fragment.
cb_step_10.detailed: This finalizes the repositioning of the adjusted fragment, integrating it into the reduction plan. Once applied, the adjustment becomes part of the working model for screw planning.

cb_step_11.title: Plan Screws
cb_step_11.simple: Click to automatically generate screw trajectories for surgical fixation.
cb_step_11.detailed: Based on the final reduced fracture configuration, this step plans optimal screw paths that secure the fragments together. The algorithm considers bone density, safe corridors, and typical fixation patterns to suggest screw models. The resulting screw trajectories will be displayed as 3D models.

cb_step_12.title: Edit Screws? (Optional)
cb_step_12.simple: Tick the checkbox to manually modify screw trajectories, or untick to finalize the plan.
cb_step_12.detailed: Automated screw paths may need refinement to avoid neurovascular structures or improve bone purchase. Ticking this box enters screw editing mode. If the suggested trajectories are acceptable, leave unticked and the workflow ends.

cb_step_13.title: Adjust Screw Trajectories
cb_step_13.simple: Use the 3D view to drag and rotate the screw trajectories into better positions.
cb_step_13.detailed: In this step you can fine-tune each screw's entry point, direction, and depth. Ensure trajectories are safely within the bone corridors and avoid critical structures. All adjustments are applied in real time to the 3D model.

cb_step_14.title: Regenerate Screws
cb_step_14.simple: Click to regenerate the final screw models from your edited trajectories.
cb_step_14.detailed: This step takes your manually adjusted trajectory lines and converts them into final screw geometries. After regeneration, the updated screws reflect your intended fixation plan.

cb_step_15.title: Disable Screw Editing
cb_step_15.simple: Untick the checkbox to exit screw editing mode and finish the workflow.
cb_step_15.detailed: Once you are satisfied with the screw adjustments, untick the edit mode to lock in the final screw positions. The planning is now complete and ready for review or export.
